_description: Citadel Lite Test Scenarios — comprehensive incident coverage
_usage: POST any scenario to /webhook/event. Each has prebuilt log data for pipeline testing.

scenarios._label: TEST: Infra Alert — Low Severity (high CPU transient spike)
scenarios.summary: Transient CPU spike on web-app-prod-01 (peak 87%, resolved)

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Alert: CPU utilization exceeded threshold
Resource: web-app-prod-01 (Standard_D4s_v3)
Metric: Percentage CPU
Condition: Greater than 80% for 5 minutes

Timeline:
  14:22:03 - CPU: 82%
  14:23:05 - CPU: 87% (peak)
  14:24:02 - CPU: 74%
  14:25:01 - CPU: 45% (resolved)

Correlation: Coincides with scheduled batch job cron-reports-daily.
No user impact detected. Response times normal.

scenarios._label: TEST: Infra Alert — Critical (database connection pool exhausted)
scenarios._expected: Block and escalate — active production impact
scenarios.summary: CRITICAL: Database connection pool exhausted, 503s in production

artifacts.log_excerpt:
CRITICAL Alert: Database Connection Pool Exhausted
Resource: db-prod-primary (Gen5, 8 vCores)
Active connections: 200/200 (100%)
Waiting queries: 847

Impact:
  HTTP 503 rate: 34% of requests (was 0.1%)
  P99 latency: 28,400ms (was 180ms)
  Affected services: api-service, auth-service, payment-service

Timeline:
  15:40:00 - Connections: 180/200
  15:41:00 - Connections: 200/200 MAXED
  15:42:00 - 503 errors begin
  15:45:00 - 847 queries waiting

Possible cause: Connection leak in api-service v3.0.0 deployed at 15:35.

scenarios._label: TEST: Security Alert — Low Severity (outdated minor dependency)
scenarios.summary: Low severity vulnerability in debug logging library

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Security Advisory: GHSA-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz
Package: debug@4.3.1
Vulnerability: Information Exposure through debug output
Severity: LOW (CVSS 3.1)
Fixed in: debug@4.3.4

Description: In development mode, debug output may include
sensitive headers when verbose logging is enabled.

Affected path: web-app > express > debug
Recommendation: Bump debug to >=4.3.4

scenarios._label: TEST: Security Alert — Medium Severity (XSS in template engine)
scenarios.summary: Medium severity XSS vulnerability in handlebars template engine

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Security Advisory: GHSA-f2jv-r9rf-7988
Package: handlebars@4.7.6
Vulnerability: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
Severity: MEDIUM (CVSS 6.1)
Fixed in: handlebars@4.7.8

Description: When using {{triple-stash}}} expressions,
handlebars fails to properly sanitize certain Unicode
control characters, allowing stored XSS attacks.

Proof of concept:
  Input: {{name}} where name = '\u0000<script>alert(1)</script>'
  Output: renders script tag

Affected path: web-app > handlebars
Recommendation: Upgrade to handlebars >=4.7.8

scenarios._label: TEST: Security Alert — Critical (RCE in serialization library)
scenarios.summary: CRITICAL: Remote Code Execution in PyYAML unsafe deserialization

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Security Advisory: GHSA-rprw-h62v-c2w7
Package: PyYAML@5.3
Vulnerability: Arbitrary Code Execution via yaml.load()
Severity: CRITICAL (CVSS 9.8)
Fixed in: PyYAML@6.0.1
CVE: CVE-2020-14343

Description: yaml.load() without Loader argument allows
execution of arbitrary Python objects through crafted YAML.

Exploit vector:
  !!python/object/apply:os.system ['rm -rf /']

Affected files:
  src/config/loader.py:42 - yaml.load(data)
  src/import/parser.py:18 - yaml.load(stream)

IMPACT: Any endpoint accepting YAML input is exploitable.
Recommendation: Upgrade and replace yaml.load() with yaml.safe_load()

scenarios._label: TEST: Rollback Needed — Medium Severity (API breaking change)
scenarios.summary: API breaking change: v3 response format breaks mobile app v2.x

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Rollback requested by @engineering-lead
Reason: Breaking API change in v3.0.0

Impact assessment:
  - /api/v2/users endpoint returns new JSON schema
  - Mobile app v2.x expects { data: [...] } format
  - v3.0.0 returns { users: [...], pagination: {...} }
  - Error rate on mobile: 67% (was 0.2%)
  - Affected users: ~12,000 on app v2.x

Previous stable: v2.9.4
Commit range: 45 commits over 3 weeks
Database migrations: 0048 applied (reversible)

scenarios._label: TEST: Rollback Needed — Critical (data corruption in production)
scenarios._expected: Block and escalate — active data loss
scenarios.summary: CRITICAL: Data corruption — v4.0.0 migration truncated user addresses

artifacts.log_excerpt:
INCIDENT REPORT — Severity: P0
Rollback requested by @VP-Engineering

Impact:
  - Migration 0052 truncated address_line_2 column to VARCHAR(10)
  - 84,291 user records affected
  - Shipping addresses corrupted for active orders
  - 1,247 orders in fulfillment pipeline with bad addresses
  - Estimated revenue impact: $340K in delayed shipments

Timeline:
  09:00 - v4.0.0 deployed to production
  09:15 - First customer complaint (wrong address)
  09:45 - Engineering confirms data truncation
  10:00 - Rollback initiated

Recovery: Requires point-in-time database restore from 08:55 backup.
WARNING: Any orders placed 09:00-10:00 will need manual reconciliation.

scenarios._label: TEST: Performance Degradation — Low Severity (slow dashboard load)
scenarios.summary: Dashboard page load time increased from 1.2s to 2.8s

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Performance Alert: Page Load Time Degradation
Page: /dashboard
Baseline P95: 1,200ms
Current P95: 2,800ms (+133%)

Breakdown:
  Server response: 180ms (normal)
  DOM load: 420ms (normal)
  JS bundle: 1,800ms (was 600ms) << REGRESSION
  API calls: 400ms (normal)

Correlation: bundle size increased from 340KB to 890KB
Commit: feat: add chart library (recharts)
Impact: No functional errors, slight UX delay only.

scenarios._label: TEST: Performance Degradation — Medium (API latency p99 spike)
scenarios.summary: API P99 latency spike: /api/search endpoint at 4.2s (baseline 200ms)

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Performance Alert: API Latency Spike
Endpoint: GET /api/search
Baseline P99: 200ms
Current P99: 4,200ms (+2000%)

Query analysis:
  Slow query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE name ILIKE '%{term}%'
  Missing index on: products.name
  Full table scan: 2.1M rows
  Avg query time: 3,800ms

Affected: 23% of API requests hit this endpoint
User-visible: Search results delayed, some timeouts
Error rate: 2.1% (timeout 503s)

Mitigation: Add GIN index on products.name, implement search caching.

scenarios._label: TEST: Performance Degradation — Critical (memory leak OOM kills)
scenarios.summary: CRITICAL: Memory leak causing OOM kills every 2 hours in production

artifacts.log_excerpt:
CRITICAL Alert: Out of Memory — Pod Restarts
Service: core-platform-api
Pod restarts in last 6h: 3 (OOMKilled)

Memory pattern:
  T+0min:   512MB (baseline)
  T+30min:  1.1GB
  T+60min:  2.3GB
  T+90min:  3.8GB
  T+120min: 4.0GB → OOMKilled (limit: 4GB)

Heap analysis (last dump before OOM):
  - EventEmitter listeners: 847,293 (leak!)
  - Unreferenced Promises: 234,891
  - String cache: 1.2GB

Root cause hypothesis: WebSocket handler not removing
event listeners on disconnect (introduced in v3.1.0).

Impact: Service restarts every ~2h, 30s downtime per restart.
All active WebSocket connections dropped on restart.

scenarios._label: TEST: Test Failure — Critical (auth bypass in security test suite)
scenarios._expected: Block — security vulnerability confirmed by tests
scenarios.summary: CRITICAL: Security test suite detected auth bypass — unauthenticated access to admin API

artifacts.log_excerpt:
Running security test suite

tests/security/test_auth.py
  test_unauthenticated_blocked ........................ PASSED
  test_expired_token_rejected ......................... PASSED
  test_invalid_signature_rejected ..................... PASSED
  test_admin_requires_admin_role ...................... FAILED!
  test_rate_limiting_enforced ......................... PASSED

FAILURE: test_admin_requires_admin_role
  Response for GET /admin/users with role='viewer':
    Expected: 403 Forbidden
    Actual:   200 OK (returned 847 user records!)

  The admin endpoint returned full user data including:
    - email addresses
    - hashed passwords
    - API keys
    - billing information

  Root cause: middleware.auth.check_role() returns True
  for any authenticated user after OAuth refactor.

  THIS IS A SECURITY VULNERABILITY. DO NOT MERGE.

4 passed, 1 failed in 8.73s
